attempt.claim: Erdos #124 correction: the reservation budget Delta_S(x)=sigma_S(x)-x is LINEAR even in the exact case sum 1/(d-1)=1 (verified ~0.8x to 1.75x for {3,4,7}), correcting the no-linear-surplus prose in att_69182d6435696069. The reservoir Budget condition sigma_R(b)<=Delta_S(b)+O(1) thus has a generous LINEAR budget. Consequence: budget size is not the bottleneck; the real obstruction is that low-mass unit identities are scarce (Diophantine a^i-b^j=1 finiteness) while the available balanced-ternary identities are super-massive, so cumulative identity prefix-mass (~1.5x, geometric) overshoots the ~0.8x worst-case budget. Sharpened core: many LOW-MASS disjoint unit identities.
